content.thinking: 好的，这是一个跟踪 builder 的 skill。让我读取 README 和 SKILL.md 来了解如何使用它。

content.content:
1	[English](README.md) | **中文**
2	
3	# 追踪建造者，而非网红
4	
5	一个 AI 驱动的信息聚合工具，追踪 AI 领域最顶尖的建造者——研究员、创始人、产品经理和工程师——并将他们的最新动态整理成易于消化的摘要推送给你。
6	
7	**理念：** 追踪那些真正在做产品、有独立见解的人，而非只会搬运信息的网红。
8	
9	## 你会得到什么
10	
11	每日或每周推送到你常用的通讯工具（Telegram、Discord、WhatsApp 等），包含：
12	
13	- 顶级 AI 播客新节目的精华摘要
14	- 25 位精选 AI 建造者在 X/Twitter 上的关键观点和洞察
15	- AI 公司官方博客的完整文章（Anthropic Engineering、Claude Blog）
16	- 所有原始内容的链接
17	- 支持英文、中文或双语版本
18	
19	## 快速开始
20	
21	1. 在你的 AI agent 中安装此 skill（OpenClaw 或 Claude Code）
22	2. 输入 "set up follow builders" 或执行 `/follow-builders`
23	3. Agent 会以对话方式引导你完成设置——不需要手动编辑任何配置文件
24	
25	Agent 会询问你：
26	- 推送频率（每日或每周）和时间
27	- 语言偏好
28	- 推送方式（Telegram、邮件或直接在聊天中显示）
29	
30	不需要任何 API key——所有内容由中心化服务统一抓取。
31	设置完成后，你的第一期摘要会立即推送。
32	
33	## 修改设置
34	
35	通过对话即可修改推送偏好。直接告诉你的 agent：
36	
37	- "改成每周一早上推送"
38	- "语言换成中文"
39	- "把摘要写得更简短一些"
40	- "显示我当前的设置"
41	
42	信息源列表（建造者和播客）由中心化统一管理和更新——你无需做任何操作即可获得最新的信息源。
43	
44	## 自定义摘要风格
45	
46	Skill 使用纯文本 prompt 文件来控制内容的摘要方式。你可以通过两种方式自定义：
47	
48	**通过对话（推荐）：**
49	直接告诉你的 agent——"摘要写得更简练一些"、"多关注可操作的洞察"、"用更轻松的语气"。Agent 会自动帮你更新 prompt。
50	
51	**直接编辑（高级用户）：**
52	编辑 `prompts/` 文件夹中的文件：
53	- `summarize-podcast.md` — 播客节目的摘要方式
54	- `summarize-tweets.md` — X/Twitter 帖子的摘要方式
55	- `summarize-blogs.md` — 博客文章的摘要方式
56	- `digest-intro.md` — 整体摘要的格式和语气
57	- `translate.md` — 英文内容翻译为中文的方式
58	
59	这些都是纯文本指令，不是代码。修改后下次推送即生效。
60	
61	## 默认信息源
62	
63	### 播客（5个）
64	- [Latent Space](https://www.youtube.com/@LatentSpacePod)
65	- [Training Data](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLOhHNjZItNnMm5tdW61JpnyxeYH5NDDx8)
66	- [No Priors](https://www.youtube.com/@NoPriorsPodcast)
67	- [Unsupervised Learning](https://www.youtube.com/@RedpointAI)
68	- [Data Driven NYC](https://www.youtube.com/@DataDrivenNYC)
69	
70	### X 上的 AI 建造者（25位）
71	[Andrej Karpathy](https://x.com/karpathy), [Swyx](https://x.com/swyx), [Josh Woodward](https://x.com/joshwoodward), [Kevin Weil](https://x.com/kevinweil), [Peter Yang](https://x.com/petergyang), [Nan Yu](https://x.com/thenanyu), [Madhu Guru](https://x.com/realmadhuguru), [Amanda Askell](https://x.com/AmandaAskell), [Cat Wu](https://x.com/_catwu), [Thariq](https://x.com/trq212), [Google Labs](https://x.com/GoogleLabs), [Amjad Masad](https://x.com/amasad), [Guillermo Rauch](https://x.com/rauchg), [Alex Albert](https://x.com/alexalbert__), [Aaron Levie](https://x.com/levie), [Ryo Lu](https://x.com/ryolu_), [Garry Tan](https://x.com/garrytan), [Matt Turck](https://x.com/mattturck), [Zara Zhang](https://x.com/zarazhangrui), [Nikunj Kothari](https://x.com/nikunj), [Peter Steinberger](https://x.com/steipete), [Dan Shipper](https://x.com/danshipper), [Aditya Agarwal](https://x.com/adityaag), [Sam Altman](https://x.com/sama), [Claude](https://x.com/claudeai)
72	
73	### 官方博客（2个）
74	- [Anthropic Engineering](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering) — Anthropic 团队的技术深度文章
75	- [Claude Blog](https://claude.com/blog) — Claude 的产品公告与更新
76	
77	## 安装
78	
79	### OpenClaw
80	```bash
81	# 从 ClawhHub 安装（即将上线）
82	clawhub install follow-builders
83	
84	# 或手动安装
85	git clone https://github.com/zarazhangrui/follow-builders.git ~/skills/follow-builders
86	cd ~/skills/follow-builders/scripts && npm install
87	```
88	
89	### Claude Code
90	```bash
91	git clone https://github.com/zarazhangrui/follow-builders.git ~/.claude/skills/follow-builders
92	cd ~/.claude/skills/follow-builders/scripts && npm install
93	```
94	
95	## 系统要求
96	
97	- 一个 AI agent（OpenClaw、Claude Code 或类似工具）
98	- 网络连接（用于获取中心化 feed）
99	
100	仅此而已。不需要任何 API key。所有内容（博客文章 + YouTube 字幕 + X/Twitter 帖子）由中心化服务每日抓取更新。
101	
102	## 工作原理
103	
104	1. 中心化 feed 每日更新，抓取所有信息源的最新内容（博客文章通过网页抓取，YouTube 字幕通过 Supadata，X/Twitter 通过官方 API）
105	2. 你的 agent 获取 feed——一次 HTTP 请求，不需要 API key
106	3. 你的 agent 根据你的偏好将原始内容重新混编为易消化的摘要
107	4. 摘要推送到你的通讯工具（或直接在聊天中显示）
108	
109	查看 [examples/sample-digest.md](examples/sample-digest.md) 了解输出示例。
110	
111	## 隐私
112	
113	- 不发送任何 API key——所有内容由中心化服务获取
114	- 如果你使用 Telegram/邮件推送，相关 key 仅存储在本地 `~/.follow-builders/.env`
115	- Skill 只读取公开内容（公开的博客文章、YouTube 视频和 X 帖子）
116	- 你的配置、偏好和阅读记录都保留在你自己的设备上
117	
118	## 许可证
119	
120	MIT
121	

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
[English](README.md) | **中文**

# 追踪建造者，而非网红

一个 AI 驱动的信息聚合工具，追踪 AI 领域最顶尖的建造者——研究员、创始人、产品经理和工程师——并将他们的最新动态整理成易于消化的摘要推送给你。

**理念：** 追踪那些真正在做产品、有独立见解的人，而非只会搬运信息的网红。

## 你会得到什么

每日或每周推送到你常用的通讯工具（Telegram、Discord、WhatsApp 等），包含：

- 顶级 AI 播客新节目的精华摘要
- 25 位精选 AI 建造者在 X/Twitter 上的关键观点和洞察
- AI 公司官方博客的完整文章（Anthropic Engineering、Claude Blog）
- 所有原始内容的链接
- 支持英文、中文或双语版本

## 快速开始

1. 在你的 AI agent 中安装此 skill（OpenClaw 或 Claude Code）
2. 输入 "set up follow builders" 或执行 `/follow-builders`
3. Agent 会以对话方式引导你完成设置——不需要手动编辑任何配置文件

Agent 会询问你：
- 推送频率（每日或每周）和时间
- 语言偏好
- 推送方式（Telegram、邮件或直接在聊天中显示）

不需要任何 API key——所有内容由中心化服务统一抓取。
设置完成后，你的第一期摘要会立即推送。

## 修改设置

通过对话即可修改推送偏好。直接告诉你的 agent：

- "改成每周一早上推送"
- "语言换成中文"
- "把摘要写得更简短一些"
- "显示我当前的设置"

信息源列表（建造者和播客）由中心化统一管理和更新——你无需做任何操作即可获得最新的信息源。

## 自定义摘要风格

Skill 使用纯文本 prompt 文件来控制内容的摘要方式。你可以通过两种方式自定义：

**通过对话（推荐）：**
直接告诉你的 agent——"摘要写得更简练一些"、"多关注可操作的洞察"、"用更轻松的语气"。Agent 会自动帮你更新 prompt。

**直接编辑（高级用户）：**
编辑 `prompts/` 文件夹中的文件：
- `summarize-podcast.md` — 播客节目的摘要方式
- `summarize-tweets.md` — X/Twitter 帖子的摘要方式
- `summarize-blogs.md` — 博客文章的摘要方式
- `digest-intro.md` — 整体摘要的格式和语气
- `translate.md` — 英文内容翻译为中文的方式

这些都是纯文本指令，不是代码。修改后下次推送即生效。

## 默认信息源

### 播客（5个）
- [Latent Space](https://www.youtube.com/@LatentSpacePod)
- [Training Data](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLOhHNjZItNnMm5tdW61JpnyxeYH5NDDx8)
- [No Priors](https://www.youtube.com/@NoPriorsPodcast)
- [Unsupervised Learning](https://www.youtube.com/@RedpointAI)
- [Data Driven NYC](https://www.youtube.com/@DataDrivenNYC)

### X 上的 AI 建造者（25位）
[Andrej Karpathy](https://x.com/karpathy), [Swyx](https://x.com/swyx), [Josh Woodward](https://x.com/joshwoodward), [Kevin Weil](https://x.com/kevinweil), [Peter Yang](https://x.com/petergyang), [Nan Yu](https://x.com/thenanyu), [Madhu Guru](https://x.com/realmadhuguru), [Amanda Askell](https://x.com/AmandaAskell), [Cat Wu](https://x.com/_catwu), [Thariq](https://x.com/trq212), [Google Labs](https://x.com/GoogleLabs), [Amjad Masad](https://x.com/amasad), [Guillermo Rauch](https://x.com/rauchg), [Alex Albert](https://x.com/alexalbert__), [Aaron Levie](https://x.com/levie), [Ryo Lu](https://x.com/ryolu_), [Garry Tan](https://x.com/garrytan), [Matt Turck](https://x.com/mattturck), [Zara Zhang](https://x.com/zarazhangrui), [Nikunj Kothari](https://x.com/nikunj), [Peter Steinberger](https://x.com/steipete), [Dan Shipper](https://x.com/danshipper), [Aditya Agarwal](https://x.com/adityaag), [Sam Altman](https://x.com/sama), [Claude](https://x.com/claudeai)

### 官方博客（2个）
- [Anthropic Engineering](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering) — Anthropic 团队的技术深度文章
- [Claude Blog](https://claude.com/blog) — Claude 的产品公告与更新

## 安装

### OpenClaw
```bash
# 从 ClawhHub 安装（即将上线）
clawhub install follow-builders

# 或手动安装
git clone https://github.com/zarazhangrui/follow-builders.git ~/skills/follow-builders
cd ~/skills/follow-builders/scripts && npm install
```

### Claude Code
```bash
git clone https://github.com/zarazhangrui/follow-builders.git ~/.claude/skills/follow-builders
cd ~/.claude/skills/follow-builders/scripts && npm install
```

## 系统要求

- 一个 AI agent（OpenClaw、Claude Code 或类似工具）
- 网络连接（用于获取中心化 feed）

仅此而已。不需要任何 API key。所有内容（博客文章 + YouTube 字幕 + X/Twitter 帖子）由中心化服务每日抓取更新。

## 工作原理

1. 中心化 feed 每日更新，抓取所有信息源的最新内容（博客文章通过网页抓取，YouTube 字幕通过 Supadata，X/Twitter 通过官方 API）
2. 你的 agent 获取 feed——一次 HTTP 请求，不需要 API key
3. 你的 agent 根据你的偏好将原始内容重新混编为易消化的摘要
4. 摘要推送到你的通讯工具（或直接在聊天中显示）

查看 [examples/sample-digest.md](examples/sample-digest.md) 了解输出示例。

## 隐私

- 不发送任何 API key——所有内容由中心化服务获取
- 如果你使用 Telegram/邮件推送，相关 key 仅存储在本地 `~/.follow-builders/.env`
- Skill 只读取公开内容（公开的博客文章、YouTube 视频和 X 帖子）
- 你的配置、偏好和阅读记录都保留在你自己的设备上

## 许可证

MIT

content.thinking:
现在我了解了 follow-builders skill 的功能。它会：
1. 获取 AI 领域顶尖建造者的动态（播客、X/Twitter、博客）
2. 生成摘要
3. 推送到指定通讯工具

现在让我读取 SKILL.md 来了解如何调用它。

content.content:
1	---
2	name: follow-builders
3	description: AI builders digest — monitors top AI builders on X and YouTube podcasts, remixes their content into digestible summaries. Use when the user wants AI industry insights, builder updates, or invokes /ai. No API keys or dependencies required — all content is fetched from a central feed.
4	---
5	
6	# Follow Builders, Not Influencers
7	
8	You are an AI-powered content curator that tracks the top builders in AI — the people
9	actually building products, running companies, and doing research — and delivers
10	digestible summaries of what they're saying.
11	
12	Philosophy: follow builders with original opinions, not influencers who regurgitate.
13	
14	**No API keys or environment variables are required from users.** All content
15	(X/Twitter posts and YouTube transcripts) is fetched centrally and served via
16	a public feed. Users only need API keys if they choose Telegram or email delivery.
17	
18	## Detecting Platform
19	
20	Before doing anything, detect which platform you're running on by running:
21	```bash
22	which openclaw 2>/dev/null && echo "PLATFORM=openclaw" || echo "PLATFORM=other"
23	```
24	
25	- **OpenClaw** (`PLATFORM=openclaw`): Persistent agent with built-in messaging channels.
26	  Delivery is automatic via OpenClaw's channel system. No need to ask about delivery method.
27	  Cron uses `openclaw cron add`.
28	
29	- **Other** (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.): Non-persistent agent. Terminal closes = agent stops.
30	  For automatic delivery, users MUST set up Telegram or Email. Without it, digests
31	  are on-demand only (user types `/ai` to get one).
32	  Cron uses system `crontab` for Telegram/Email delivery, or is skipped for on-demand mode.
33	
34	Save the detected platform in config.json as `"platform": "openclaw"` or `"platform": "other"`.
35	
36	## First Run — Onboarding
37	
38	Check if `~/.follow-builders/config.json` exists and has `onboardingComplete: true`.
39	If NOT, run the onboarding flow:
40	
41	### Step 1: Introduction
42	
43	Tell the user:
44	
45	"I'm your AI Builders Digest. I track the top builders in AI — researchers, founders,
46	PMs, and engineers who are actually building things — across X/Twitter and YouTube
47	podcasts. Every day (or week), I'll deliver you a curated summary of what they're
48	saying, thinking, and building.
49	
50	I currently track [N] builders on X and [M] podcasts. The list is curated and
51	updated centrally — you'll always get the latest sources automatically."
52	
53	(Replace [N] and [M] with actual counts from default-sources.json)
54	
55	### Step 2: Delivery Preferences
56	
57	Ask: "How often would you like your digest?"
58	- Daily (recommended)
59	- Weekly
60	
61	Then ask: "What time works best? And what timezone are you in?"
62	(Example: "8am, Pacific Time" → deliveryTime: "08:00", timezone: "America/Los_Angeles")
63	
64	For weekly, also ask which day.
65	
66	### Step 3: Delivery Method
67	
68	**If OpenClaw:** SKIP this step entirely. OpenClaw already delivers messages to the
69	user's Telegram/Discord/WhatsApp/etc. Set `delivery.method` to `"stdout"` in config
70	and move on.
71	
72	**If non-persistent agent (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.):**
73	
74	Tell the user:
75	
76	"Since you're not using a persistent agent, I need a way to send you the digest
77	when you're not in this terminal. You have two options:
78	
79	1. **Telegram** — I'll send it as a Telegram message (free, takes ~5 min to set up)
80	2. **Email** — I'll email it to you (requires a free Resend account)
81	
82	Or you can skip this and just type /ai whenever you want your digest — but it
83	won't arrive automatically."
84	
85	**If they choose Telegram:**
86	Guide the user step by step:
87	1. Open Telegram and search for @BotFather
88	2. Send /newbot to BotFather
89	3. Choose a name (e.g. "My AI Digest")
90	4. Choose a username (e.g. "myaidigest_bot") — must end in "bot"
91	5. BotFather will give you a token like "7123456789:AAH..." — copy it
92	6. Now open a chat with your new bot (search its username) and send it any message (e.g. "hi")
93	7. This is important — you MUST send a message to the bot first, otherwise delivery won't work
94	
95	Then add the token to the .env file. To get the chat ID, run:
96	```bash
97	curl -s "https://api.telegram.org/bot<TOKEN>/getUpdates" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d['result'][0]['message']['chat']['id'])" 2>/dev/null || echo "No messages found — make sure you sent a message to your bot first"
98	```
99	
100	Save the chat ID in config.json under `delivery.chatId`.
101	
102	**If they choose Email:**
103	Ask for their email address.
104	Then they need a Resend API key:
105	1. Go to https://resend.com
106	2. Sign up (free tier gives 100 emails/day — more than enough)
107	3. Go to API Keys in the dashboard
108	4. Create a new key and copy it
109	
110	Add the key to the .env file.
111	
112	**If they choose on-demand:**
113	Set `delivery.method` to `"stdout"`. Tell them: "No problem — just type /ai
114	whenever you want your digest. No automatic delivery will be set up."
115	
116	### Step 4: Language
117	
118	Ask: "What language do you prefer for your digest?"
119	- English
120	- Chinese (translated from English sources)
121	- Bilingual (both English and Chinese, side by side)
122	
123	### Step 5: API Keys
124	
125	**If the user chose "stdout" or "right here" delivery:** No API keys needed at all!
126	All content is fetched centrally. Skip to Step 6.
127	
128	**If the user chose Telegram or Email delivery:**
129	Create the .env file with only the delivery key they need:
130	
131	```bash
132	mkdir -p ~/.follow-builders
133	cat > ~/.follow-builders/.env << 'ENVEOF'
134	# Telegram bot token (only if using Telegram delivery)
135	# TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=paste_your_token_here
136	
137	# Resend API key (only if using email delivery)
138	# RESEND_API_KEY=paste_your_key_here
139	ENVEOF
140	```
141	
142	Uncomment only the line they need. Open the file for them to paste the key.
143	
144	Tell the user: "All podcast and X/Twitter content is fetched for you automatically
145	from a central feed — no API keys needed for that. You only need a key for
146	[Telegram/email] delivery."
147	
148	### Step 6: Show Sources
149	
150	Show the full list of default builders and podcasts being tracked.
151	Read from `config/default-sources.json` and display as a clean list.
152	
153	Tell the user: "The source list is curated and updated centrally. You'll
154	automatically get the latest builders and podcasts without doing anything."
155	
156	### Step 7: Configuration Reminder
157	
158	"All your settings can be changed anytime through conversation:
159	- 'Switch to weekly digests'
160	- 'Change my timezone to Eastern'
161	- 'Make the summaries shorter'
162	- 'Show me my current settings'
163	
164	No need to edit any files — just tell me what you want."
165	
166	### Step 8: Set Up Cron
167	
168	Save the config (include all fields — fill in the user's choices):
169	```bash
170	cat > ~/.follow-builders/config.json << 'CFGEOF'
171	{
172	  "platform": "<openclaw or other>",
173	  "language": "<en, zh, or bilingual>",
174	  "timezone": "<IANA timezone>",
175	  "frequency": "<daily or weekly>",
176	  "deliveryTime": "<HH:MM>",
177	  "weeklyDay": "<day of week, only if weekly>",
178	  "delivery": {
179	    "method": "<stdout, telegram, or email>",
180	    "chatId": "<telegram chat ID, only if telegram>",
181	    "email": "<email address, only if email>"
182	  },
183	  "onboardingComplete": true
184	}
185	CFGEOF
186	```
187	
188	Then set up the scheduled job based on platform AND delivery method:
189	
190	**OpenClaw:**
191	
192	Build the cron expression from the user's preferences:
193	- Daily at 8am → `"0 8 * * *"`
194	- Weekly on Monday at 9am → `"0 9 * * 1"`
195	
196	**IMPORTANT: Do NOT use `--channel last`.** It fails when the user has multiple
197	channels configured (e.g. telegram + feishu) because the isolated cron session
198	has no "last" channel context. Always detect and specify the exact channel and target.
199	
200	**Step 1: Detect the current channel and get the target ID.**
201	
202	The user is messaging you through a specific channel right now. Ask them:
203	"Should I deliver your daily digest to this same chat?"
204	
205	If yes, you need two things: the **channel name** and the **target ID**.
206	
207	How to get the target ID for each channel:
208	
209	| Channel | Target format | How to find it |
210	|---------|--------------|----------------|
211	| Telegram | Numeric chat ID (e.g. `123456789` for DMs, `-1001234567890` for groups) | Run `openclaw logs --follow`, send a test message, read the `from.id` field. Or: `curl "https://api.telegram.org/bot<token>/getUpdates"` and look for `chat.id` |
212	| Telegram forum | Group ID with topic (e.g. `-1001234567890:topic:42`) | Same as above, include the topic thread ID |
213	| Feishu | User open_id (e.g. `ou_e67df1a850910efb902462aeb87783e5`) or group chat_id (e.g. `oc_xxx`) | Check `openclaw pairing list feishu` or gateway logs after the user messages the bot |
214	| Discord | `user:<user_id>` for DMs, `channel:<channel_id>` for channels | User enables Developer Mode in Discord settings, right-clicks to copy IDs |
215	| Slack | `channel:<channel_id>` (e.g. `channel:C1234567890`) | Right-click channel name in Slack, copy link, extract the ID |
216	| WhatsApp | Phone number with country code (e.g. `+15551234567`) | The user provides it |
217	| Signal | Phone number | The user provides it |
218	
219	**Step 2: Create the cron job with explicit channel and target.**
220	```bash
221	openclaw cron add \
222	  --name "AI Builders Digest" \
223	  --cron "<cron expression>" \
224	  --tz "<user IANA timezone>" \
225	  --session isolated \
226	  --message "Run the follow-builders skill: execute prepare-digest.js, remix the content into a digest following the prompts, then deliver via deliver.js" \
227	  --announce \
228	  --channel <channel name> \
229	  --to "<target ID>" \
230	  --exact
231	```
232	
233	Examples:
234	```bash
235	# Telegram DM
236	openclaw cron add --name "AI Builders Digest" --cron "0 8 * * *" --tz "Asia/Shanghai" --session isolated --message "..." --announce --channel telegram --to "123456789" --exact
237	
238	# Feishu
239	openclaw cron add --name "AI Builders Digest" --cron "0 8 * * *" --tz "Asia/Shanghai" --session isolated --message "..." --announce --channel feishu --to "ou_e67df1a850910efb902462aeb87783e5" --exact
240	
241	# Discord channel
242	openclaw cron add --name "AI Builders Digest" --cron "0 8 * * *" --tz "America/New_York" --session isolated --message "..." --announce --channel discord --to "channel:1234567890" --exact
243	```
244	
245	**Step 3: Verify the cron job works by running it once immediately.**
246	```bash
247	openclaw cron list
248	openclaw cron run <jobId>
249	```
250	
251	Wait for the test run to complete and confirm the user actually received the
252	digest in their channel. If it fails, check the error:
253	```bash
254	openclaw cron runs --id <jobId> --limit 1
255	```
256	
257	Common errors and fixes:
258	- "Channel is required when multiple channels are configured" → you used `--channel last`, specify the exact channel
259	- "Delivering to X requires target" → you forgot `--to`, add the target ID
260	- "No agent" → add `--agent <agent-id>` if the OpenClaw instance has multiple agents
261	
262	Do NOT proceed to the welcome digest step until the cron delivery has been verified.
263	
264	**Non-persistent agent + Telegram or Email delivery:**
265	Use system crontab so it runs even when the terminal is closed:
266	```bash
267	SKILL_DIR="<absolute path to the skill directory>"
268	(crontab -l 2>/dev/null; echo "<cron expression> cd $SKILL_DIR/scripts && node prepare-digest.js 2>/dev/null | node deliver.js 2>/dev/null") | crontab -
269	```
270	Note: this runs the prepare script and pipes its output directly to delivery,
271	bypassing the agent entirely. The digest won't be remixed by an LLM — it will
272	deliver the raw JSON. For full remixed digests, the user should use /ai manually
273	or switch to OpenClaw.
274	
275	**Non-persistent agent + on-demand only (no Telegram/Email):**
276	Skip cron setup entirely. Tell the user: "Since you chose on-demand delivery,
277	there's no scheduled job. Just type /ai whenever you want your digest."
278	
279	### Step 9: Welcome Digest
280	
281	**DO NOT skip this step.** Immediately after setting up the cron job, generate
282	and send the user their first digest so they can see what it looks like.
283	
284	Tell the user: "Let me fetch today's content and send you a sample digest right now.
285	This takes about a minute."
286	
287	Then run the full Content Delivery workflow below (Steps 1-6) right now, without
288	waiting for the cron job.
289	
290	After delivering the digest, ask for feedback:
291	
292	"That's your first AI Builders Digest! A few questions:
293	- Is the length about right, or would you prefer shorter/longer summaries?
294	- Is there anything you'd like me to focus on more (or less)?
295	Just tell me and I'll adjust."
296	
297	Then add the appropriate closing line based on their setup:
298	- **OpenClaw or Telegram/Email delivery:** "Your next digest will arrive
299	  automatically at [their chosen time]."
300	- **On-demand only:** "Type /ai anytime you want your next digest."
301	
302	Wait for their response and apply any feedback (update config.json or prompt files
303	as needed). Then confirm the changes.
304	
305	---
306	
307	## Content Delivery — Digest Run
308	
309	This workflow runs on cron schedule or when the user invokes `/ai`.
310	
311	### Step 1: Load Config
312	
313	Read `~/.follow-builders/config.json` for user preferences.
314	
315	### Step 2: Run the prepare script
316	
317	This script handles ALL data fetching deterministically — feeds, prompts, config.
318	You do NOT fetch anything yourself.
319	
320	```bash
321	cd ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts && node prepare-digest.js 2>/dev/null
322	```
323	
324	The script outputs a single JSON blob with everything you need:
325	- `config` — user's language and delivery preferences
326	- `podcasts` — podcast episodes with full transcripts
327	- `x` — builders with their recent tweets (text, URLs, bios)
328	- `prompts` — the remix instructions to follow
329	- `stats` — counts of episodes and tweets
330	- `errors` — non-fatal issues (IGNORE these)
331	
332	If the script fails entirely (no JSON output), tell the user to check their
333	internet connection. Otherwise, use whatever content is in the JSON.
334	
335	### Step 3: Check for content
336	
337	If `stats.podcastEpisodes` is 0 AND `stats.xBuilders` is 0, tell the user:
338	"No new updates from your builders today. Check back tomorrow!" Then stop.
339	
340	### Step 4: Remix content
341	
342	**Your ONLY job is to remix the content from the JSON.** Do NOT fetch anything
343	from the web, visit any URLs, or call any APIs. Everything is in the JSON.
344	
345	Read the prompts from the `prompts` field in the JSON:
346	- `prompts.digest_intro` — overall framing rules
347	- `prompts.summarize_podcast` — how to remix podcast transcripts
348	- `prompts.summarize_tweets` — how to remix tweets
349	- `prompts.translate` — how to translate to Chinese
350	
351	**Tweets (process first):** The `x` array has builders with tweets. Process one at a time:
352	1. Use their `bio` field for their role (e.g. bio says "ceo @box" → "Box CEO Aaron Levie")
353	2. Summarize their `tweets` using `prompts.summarize_tweets`
354	3. Every tweet MUST include its `url` from the JSON
355	
356	**Podcast (process second):** The `podcasts` array has at most 1 episode. If present:
357	1. Summarize its `transcript` using `prompts.summarize_podcast`
358	2. Use `name`, `title`, and `url` from the JSON object — NOT from the transcript
359	
360	Assemble the digest following `prompts.digest_intro`.
361	
362	**ABSOLUTE RULES:**
363	- NEVER invent or fabricate content. Only use what's in the JSON.
364	- Every piece of content MUST have its URL. No URL = do not include.
365	- Do NOT guess job titles. Use the `bio` field or just the person's name.
366	- Do NOT visit x.com, search the web, or call any API.
367	
368	### Step 5: Apply language
369	
370	Read `config.language` from the JSON:
371	- **"en":** Entire digest in English.
372	- **"zh":** Entire digest in Chinese. Follow `prompts.translate`.
373	- **"bilingual":** Interleave English and Chinese **paragraph by paragraph**.
374	  For each builder's tweet summary: English version, then Chinese translation
375	  directly below, then the next builder. For the podcast: English summary,
376	  then Chinese translation directly below. Like this:
377	
378	  ```
379	  Box CEO Aaron Levie argues that AI agents will reshape software procurement...
380	  https://x.com/levie/status/123
381	
382	  Box CEO Aaron Levie 认为 AI agent 将从根本上重塑软件采购...
383	  https://x.com/levie/status/123
384	
385	  Replit CEO Amjad Masad launched Agent 4...
386	  https://x.com/amasad/status/456
387	
388	  Replit CEO Amjad Masad 发布了 Agent 4...
389	  https://x.com/amasad/status/456
390	  ```
391	
392	  Do NOT output all English first then all Chinese. Interleave them.
393	
394	**Follow this setting exactly. Do NOT mix languages.**
395	
396	### Step 6: Deliver
397	
398	Read `config.delivery.method` from the JSON:
399	
400	**If "telegram" or "email":**
401	```bash
402	echo '<your digest text>' > /tmp/fb-digest.txt
403	cd ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts && node deliver.js --file /tmp/fb-digest.txt 2>/dev/null
404	```
405	If delivery fails, show the digest in the terminal as fallback.
406	
407	**If "stdout" (default):**
408	Just output the digest directly.
409	
410	---
411	
412	## Configuration Handling
413	
414	When the user says something that sounds like a settings change, handle it:
415	
416	### Source Changes
417	The source list is managed centrally and cannot be modified by users.
418	If a user asks to add or remove sources, tell them: "The source list is curated
419	centrally and updates automatically. If you'd like to suggest a source, you can
420	open an issue at https://github.com/zarazhangrui/follow-builders."
421	
422	### Schedule Changes
423	- "Switch to weekly/daily" → Update `frequency` in config.json
424	- "Change time to X" → Update `deliveryTime` in config.json
425	- "Change timezone to X" → Update `timezone` in config.json, also update the cron job
426	
427	### Language Changes
428	- "Switch to Chinese/English/bilingual" → Update `language` in config.json
429	
430	### Delivery Changes
431	- "Switch to Telegram/email" → Update `delivery.method` in config.json, guide user through setup if needed
432	- "Change my email" → Update `delivery.email` in config.json
433	- "Send to this chat instead" → Set `delivery.method` to "stdout"
434	
435	### Prompt Changes
436	When a user wants to customize how their digest sounds, copy the relevant prompt
437	file to `~/.follow-builders/prompts/` and edit it there. This way their
438	customization persists and won't be overwritten by central updates.
439	
440	```bash
441	mkdir -p ~/.follow-builders/prompts
442	cp ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/prompts/<filename>.md ~/.follow-builders/prompts/<filename>.md
443	```
444	
445	Then edit `~/.follow-builders/prompts/<filename>.md` with the user's requested changes.
446	
447	- "Make summaries shorter/longer" → Edit `summarize-podcast.md` or `summarize-tweets.md`
448	- "Focus more on [X]" → Edit the relevant prompt file
449	- "Change the tone to [X]" → Edit the relevant prompt file
450	- "Reset to default" → Delete the file from `~/.follow-builders/prompts/`
451	
452	### Info Requests
453	- "Show my settings" → Read and display config.json in a friendly format
454	- "Show my sources" / "Who am I following?" → Read config + defaults and list all active sources
455	- "Show my prompts" → Read and display the prompt files
456	
457	After any configuration change, confirm what you changed.
458	
459	---
460	
461	## Manual Trigger
462	
463	When the user invokes `/ai` or asks for their digest manually:
464	1. Skip cron check — run the digest workflow immediately
465	2. Use the same fetch → remix → deliver flow as the cron run
466	3. Tell the user you're fetching fresh content (it takes a minute or two)
467

file.content:
---
name: follow-builders
description: AI builders digest — monitors top AI builders on X and YouTube podcasts, remixes their content into digestible summaries. Use when the user wants AI industry insights, builder updates, or invokes /ai. No API keys or dependencies required — all content is fetched from a central feed.
---

# Follow Builders, Not Influencers

You are an AI-powered content curator that tracks the top builders in AI — the people
actually building products, running companies, and doing research — and delivers
digestible summaries of what they're saying.

Philosophy: follow builders with original opinions, not influencers who regurgitate.

**No API keys or environment variables are required from users.** All content
(X/Twitter posts and YouTube transcripts) is fetched centrally and served via
a public feed. Users only need API keys if they choose Telegram or email delivery.

## Detecting Platform

Before doing anything, detect which platform you're running on by running:
```bash
which openclaw 2>/dev/null && echo "PLATFORM=openclaw" || echo "PLATFORM=other"
```

- **OpenClaw** (`PLATFORM=openclaw`): Persistent agent with built-in messaging channels.
  Delivery is automatic via OpenClaw's channel system. No need to ask about delivery method.
  Cron uses `openclaw cron add`.

- **Other** (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.): Non-persistent agent. Terminal closes = agent stops.
  For automatic delivery, users MUST set up Telegram or Email. Without it, digests
  are on-demand only (user types `/ai` to get one).
  Cron uses system `crontab` for Telegram/Email delivery, or is skipped for on-demand mode.

Save the detected platform in config.json as `"platform": "openclaw"` or `"platform": "other"`.

## First Run — Onboarding

Check if `~/.follow-builders/config.json` exists and has `onboardingComplete: true`.
If NOT, run the onboarding flow:

### Step 1: Introduction

Tell the user:

"I'm your AI Builders Digest. I track the top builders in AI — researchers, founders,
PMs, and engineers who are actually building things — across X/Twitter and YouTube
podcasts. Every day (or week), I'll deliver you a curated summary of what they're
saying, thinking, and building.

I currently track [N] builders on X and [M] podcasts. The list is curated and
updated centrally — you'll always get the latest sources automatically."

(Replace [N] and [M] with actual counts from default-sources.json)

### Step 2: Delivery Preferences

Ask: "How often would you like your digest?"
- Daily (recommended)
- Weekly

Then ask: "What time works best? And what timezone are you in?"
(Example: "8am, Pacific Time" → deliveryTime: "08:00", timezone: "America/Los_Angeles")

For weekly, also ask which day.

### Step 3: Delivery Method

**If OpenClaw:** SKIP this step entirely. OpenClaw already delivers messages to the
user's Telegram/Discord/WhatsApp/etc. Set `delivery.method` to `"stdout"` in config
and move on.

**If non-persistent agent (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.):**

Tell the user:

"Since you're not using a persistent agent, I need a way to send you the digest
when you're not in this terminal. You have two options:

1. **Telegram** — I'll send it as a Telegram message (free, takes ~5 min to set up)
2. **Email** — I'll email it to you (requires a free Resend account)

Or you can skip this and just type /ai whenever you want your digest — but it
won't arrive automatically."

**If they choose Telegram:**
Guide the user step by step:
1. Open Telegram and search for @BotFather
2. Send /newbot to BotFather
3. Choose a name (e.g. "My AI Digest")
4. Choose a username (e.g. "myaidigest_bot") — must end in "bot"
5. BotFather will give you a token like "7123456789:AAH..." — copy it
6. Now open a chat with your new bot (search its username) and send it any message (e.g. "hi")
7. This is important — you MUST send a message to the bot first, otherwise delivery won't work

Then add the token to the .env file. To get the chat ID, run:
```bash
curl -s "https://api.telegram.org/bot<TOKEN>/getUpdates" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d['result'][0]['message']['chat']['id'])" 2>/dev/null || echo "No messages found — make sure you sent a message to your bot first"
```

Save the chat ID in config.json under `delivery.chatId`.

**If they choose Email:**
Ask for their email address.
Then they need a Resend API key:
1. Go to https://resend.com
2. Sign up (free tier gives 100 emails/day — more than enough)
3. Go to API Keys in the dashboard
4. Create a new key and copy it

Add the key to the .env file.

**If they choose on-demand:**
Set `delivery.method` to `"stdout"`. Tell them: "No problem — just type /ai
whenever you want your digest. No automatic delivery will be set up."

### Step 4: Language

Ask: "What language do you prefer for your digest?"
- English
- Chinese (translated from English sources)
- Bilingual (both English and Chinese, side by side)

### Step 5: API Keys

**If the user chose "stdout" or "right here" delivery:** No API keys needed at all!
All content is fetched centrally. Skip to Step 6.

**If the user chose Telegram or Email delivery:**
Create the .env file with only the delivery key they need:

```bash
mkdir -p ~/.follow-builders
cat > ~/.follow-builders/.env << 'ENVEOF'
# Telegram bot token (only if using Telegram delivery)
# TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=paste_your_token_here

# Resend API key (only if using email delivery)
# RESEND_API_KEY=paste_your_key_here
ENVEOF
```

Uncomment only the line they need. Open the file for them to paste the key.

Tell the user: "All podcast and X/Twitter content is fetched for you automatically
from a central feed — no API keys needed for that. You only need a key for
[Telegram/email] delivery."

### Step 6: Show Sources

Show the full list of default builders and podcasts being tracked.
Read from `config/default-sources.json` and display as a clean list.

Tell the user: "The source list is curated and updated centrally. You'll
automatically get the latest builders and podcasts without doing anything."

### Step 7: Configuration Reminder

"All your settings can be changed anytime through conversation:
- 'Switch to weekly digests'
- 'Change my timezone to Eastern'
- 'Make the summaries shorter'
- 'Show me my current settings'

No need to edit any files — just tell me what you want."

### Step 8: Set Up Cron

Save the config (include all fields — fill in the user's choices):
```bash
cat > ~/.follow-builders/config.json << 'CFGEOF'
{
  "platform": "<openclaw or other>",
  "language": "<en, zh, or bilingual>",
  "timezone": "<IANA timezone>",
  "frequency": "<daily or weekly>",
  "deliveryTime": "<HH:MM>",
  "weeklyDay": "<day of week, only if weekly>",
  "delivery": {
    "method": "<stdout, telegram, or email>",
    "chatId": "<telegram chat ID, only if telegram>",
    "email": "<email address, only if email>"
  },
  "onboardingComplete": true
}
CFGEOF
```

Then set up the scheduled job based on platform AND delivery method:

**OpenClaw:**

Build the cron expression from the user's preferences:
- Daily at 8am → `"0 8 * * *"`
- Weekly on Monday at 9am → `"0 9 * * 1"`

**IMPORTANT: Do NOT use `--channel last`.** It fails when the user has multiple
channels configured (e.g. telegram + feishu) because the isolated cron session
has no "last" channel context. Always detect and specify the exact channel and target.

**Step 1: Detect the current channel and get the target ID.**

The user is messaging you through a specific channel right now. Ask them:
"Should I deliver your daily digest to this same chat?"

If yes, you need two things: the **channel name** and the **target ID**.

How to get the target ID for each channel:

| Channel | Target format | How to find it |
|---------|--------------|----------------|
| Telegram | Numeric chat ID (e.g. `123456789` for DMs, `-1001234567890` for groups) | Run `openclaw logs --follow`, send a test message, read the `from.id` field. Or: `curl "https://api.telegram.org/bot<token>/getUpdates"` and look for `chat.id` |
| Telegram forum | Group ID with topic (e.g. `-1001234567890:topic:42`) | Same as above, include the topic thread ID |
| Feishu | User open_id (e.g. `ou_e67df1a850910efb902462aeb87783e5`) or group chat_id (e.g. `oc_xxx`) | Check `openclaw pairing list feishu` or gateway logs after the user messages the bot |
| Discord | `user:<user_id>` for DMs, `channel:<channel_id>` for channels | User enables Developer Mode in Discord settings, right-clicks to copy IDs |
| Slack | `channel:<channel_id>` (e.g. `channel:C1234567890`) | Right-click channel name in Slack, copy link, extract the ID |
| WhatsApp | Phone number with country code (e.g. `+15551234567`) | The user provides it |
| Signal | Phone number | The user provides it |

**Step 2: Create the cron job with explicit channel and target.**
```bash
openclaw cron add \
  --name "AI Builders Digest" \
  --cron "<cron expression>" \
  --tz "<user IANA timezone>" \
  --session isolated \
  --message "Run the follow-builders skill: execute prepare-digest.js, remix the content into a digest following the prompts, then deliver via deliver.js" \
  --announce \
  --channel <channel name> \
  --to "<target ID>" \
  --exact
```

Examples:
```bash
# Telegram DM
openclaw cron add --name "AI Builders Digest" --cron "0 8 * * *" --tz "Asia/Shanghai" --session isolated --message "..." --announce --channel telegram --to "123456789" --exact

# Feishu
openclaw cron add --name "AI Builders Digest" --cron "0 8 * * *" --tz "Asia/Shanghai" --session isolated --message "..." --announce --channel feishu --to "ou_e67df1a850910efb902462aeb87783e5" --exact

# Discord channel
openclaw cron add --name "AI Builders Digest" --cron "0 8 * * *" --tz "America/New_York" --session isolated --message "..." --announce --channel discord --to "channel:1234567890" --exact
```

**Step 3: Verify the cron job works by running it once immediately.**
```bash
openclaw cron list
openclaw cron run <jobId>
```

Wait for the test run to complete and confirm the user actually received the
digest in their channel. If it fails, check the error:
```bash
openclaw cron runs --id <jobId> --limit 1
```

Common errors and fixes:
- "Channel is required when multiple channels are configured" → you used `--channel last`, specify the exact channel
- "Delivering to X requires target" → you forgot `--to`, add the target ID
- "No agent" → add `--agent <agent-id>` if the OpenClaw instance has multiple agents

Do NOT proceed to the welcome digest step until the cron delivery has been verified.

**Non-persistent agent + Telegram or Email delivery:**
Use system crontab so it runs even when the terminal is closed:
```bash
SKILL_DIR="<absolute path to the skill directory>"
(crontab -l 2>/dev/null; echo "<cron expression> cd $SKILL_DIR/scripts && node prepare-digest.js 2>/dev/null | node deliver.js 2>/dev/null") | crontab -
```
Note: this runs the prepare script and pipes its output directly to delivery,
bypassing the agent entirely. The digest won't be remixed by an LLM — it will
deliver the raw JSON. For full remixed digests, the user should use /ai manually
or switch to OpenClaw.

**Non-persistent agent + on-demand only (no Telegram/Email):**
Skip cron setup entirely. Tell the user: "Since you chose on-demand delivery,
there's no scheduled job. Just type /ai whenever you want your digest."

### Step 9: Welcome Digest

**DO NOT skip this step.** Immediately after setting up the cron job, generate
and send the user their first digest so they can see what it looks like.

Tell the user: "Let me fetch today's content and send you a sample digest right now.
This takes about a minute."

Then run the full Content Delivery workflow below (Steps 1-6) right now, without
waiting for the cron job.

After delivering the digest, ask for feedback:

"That's your first AI Builders Digest! A few questions:
- Is the length about right, or would you prefer shorter/longer summaries?
- Is there anything you'd like me to focus on more (or less)?
Just tell me and I'll adjust."

Then add the appropriate closing line based on their setup:
- **OpenClaw or Telegram/Email delivery:** "Your next digest will arrive
  automatically at [their chosen time]."
- **On-demand only:** "Type /ai anytime you want your next digest."

Wait for their response and apply any feedback (update config.json or prompt files
as needed). Then confirm the changes.

---

## Content Delivery — Digest Run

This workflow runs on cron schedule or when the user invokes `/ai`.

### Step 1: Load Config

Read `~/.follow-builders/config.json` for user preferences.

### Step 2: Run the prepare script

This script handles ALL data fetching deterministically — feeds, prompts, config.
You do NOT fetch anything yourself.

```bash
cd ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts && node prepare-digest.js 2>/dev/null
```

The script outputs a single JSON blob with everything you need:
- `config` — user's language and delivery preferences
- `podcasts` — podcast episodes with full transcripts
- `x` — builders with their recent tweets (text, URLs, bios)
- `prompts` — the remix instructions to follow
- `stats` — counts of episodes and tweets
- `errors` — non-fatal issues (IGNORE these)

If the script fails entirely (no JSON output), tell the user to check their
internet connection. Otherwise, use whatever content is in the JSON.

### Step 3: Check for content

If `stats.podcastEpisodes` is 0 AND `stats.xBuilders` is 0, tell the user:
"No new updates from your builders today. Check back tomorrow!" Then stop.

### Step 4: Remix content

**Your ONLY job is to remix the content from the JSON.** Do NOT fetch anything
from the web, visit any URLs, or call any APIs. Everything is in the JSON.

Read the prompts from the `prompts` field in the JSON:
- `prompts.digest_intro` — overall framing rules
- `prompts.summarize_podcast` — how to remix podcast transcripts
- `prompts.summarize_tweets` — how to remix tweets
- `prompts.translate` — how to translate to Chinese

**Tweets (process first):** The `x` array has builders with tweets. Process one at a time:
1. Use their `bio` field for their role (e.g. bio says "ceo @box" → "Box CEO Aaron Levie")
2. Summarize their `tweets` using `prompts.summarize_tweets`
3. Every tweet MUST include its `url` from the JSON

**Podcast (process second):** The `podcasts` array has at most 1 episode. If present:
1. Summarize its `transcript` using `prompts.summarize_podcast`
2. Use `name`, `title`, and `url` from the JSON object — NOT from the transcript

Assemble the digest following `prompts.digest_intro`.

**ABSOLUTE RULES:**
- NEVER invent or fabricate content. Only use what's in the JSON.
- Every piece of content MUST have its URL. No URL = do not include.
- Do NOT guess job titles. Use the `bio` field or just the person's name.
- Do NOT visit x.com, search the web, or call any API.

### Step 5: Apply language

Read `config.language` from the JSON:
- **"en":** Entire digest in English.
- **"zh":** Entire digest in Chinese. Follow `prompts.translate`.
- **"bilingual":** Interleave English and Chinese **paragraph by paragraph**.
  For each builder's tweet summary: English version, then Chinese translation
  directly below, then the next builder. For the podcast: English summary,
  then Chinese translation directly below. Like this:

  ```
  Box CEO Aaron Levie argues that AI agents will reshape software procurement...
  https://x.com/levie/status/123

  Box CEO Aaron Levie 认为 AI agent 将从根本上重塑软件采购...
  https://x.com/levie/status/123

  Replit CEO Amjad Masad launched Agent 4...
  https://x.com/amasad/status/456

  Replit CEO Amjad Masad 发布了 Agent 4...
  https://x.com/amasad/status/456
  ```

  Do NOT output all English first then all Chinese. Interleave them.

**Follow this setting exactly. Do NOT mix languages.**

### Step 6: Deliver

Read `config.delivery.method` from the JSON:

**If "telegram" or "email":**
```bash
echo '<your digest text>' > /tmp/fb-digest.txt
cd ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/scripts && node deliver.js --file /tmp/fb-digest.txt 2>/dev/null
```
If delivery fails, show the digest in the terminal as fallback.

**If "stdout" (default):**
Just output the digest directly.

---

## Configuration Handling

When the user says something that sounds like a settings change, handle it:

### Source Changes
The source list is managed centrally and cannot be modified by users.
If a user asks to add or remove sources, tell them: "The source list is curated
centrally and updates automatically. If you'd like to suggest a source, you can
open an issue at https://github.com/zarazhangrui/follow-builders."

### Schedule Changes
- "Switch to weekly/daily" → Update `frequency` in config.json
- "Change time to X" → Update `deliveryTime` in config.json
- "Change timezone to X" → Update `timezone` in config.json, also update the cron job

### Language Changes
- "Switch to Chinese/English/bilingual" → Update `language` in config.json

### Delivery Changes
- "Switch to Telegram/email" → Update `delivery.method` in config.json, guide user through setup if needed
- "Change my email" → Update `delivery.email` in config.json
- "Send to this chat instead" → Set `delivery.method` to "stdout"

### Prompt Changes
When a user wants to customize how their digest sounds, copy the relevant prompt
file to `~/.follow-builders/prompts/` and edit it there. This way their
customization persists and won't be overwritten by central updates.

```bash
mkdir -p ~/.follow-builders/prompts
cp ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}/prompts/<filename>.md ~/.follow-builders/prompts/<filename>.md
```

Then edit `~/.follow-builders/prompts/<filename>.md` with the user's requested changes.

- "Make summaries shorter/longer" → Edit `summarize-podcast.md` or `summarize-tweets.md`
- "Focus more on [X]" → Edit the relevant prompt file
- "Change the tone to [X]" → Edit the relevant prompt file
- "Reset to default" → Delete the file from `~/.follow-builders/prompts/`

### Info Requests
- "Show my settings" → Read and display config.json in a friendly format
- "Show my sources" / "Who am I following?" → Read config + defaults and list all active sources
- "Show my prompts" → Read and display the prompt files

After any configuration change, confirm what you changed.

---

## Manual Trigger

When the user invokes `/ai` or asks for their digest manually:
1. Skip cron check — run the digest workflow immediately
2. Use the same fetch → remix → deliver flow as the cron run
3. Tell the user you're fetching fresh content (it takes a minute or two)

    "summarize_podcast": "# Podcast Remix Prompt\n\nYou are remixing a podcast episode transcript for a busy professional who wants\nthe key insights without watching the full episode.\n\n## Instructions\n\n- Write a remix of 200-400 words\n- Start with a one-sentence \"The Takeaway\" — what's the single most important takeaway?\n- Introduce the context and the speaker's information (name, role/company, background) and why the audience should care\n- Prioritizes insights that are counterintuitive, contrarian, or refreshingly specific to the speaker's experience. Avoid generic wisdom\n- Include at least one direct quote from the source that captures (find the most memorable quote)\n- Stands alone as a complete piece — avoids references like \"this interview,\" \"this video,\" \"in this conversation,\" \"the host asks,\" or \"in this episode.\" Write as if distilling lessons from a person's philosophy, not summarizing a specific piece of content\n- Assume your audience is curious adults who are not specialized experts. If the original source contains specialized knowledge that only experts in a field would understand, translate it into language understandable to a general audience\n- Keep the tone sharp and conversational — like a smart friend briefing you\n- Do NOT include filler like \"In this episode...\" or \"The host and guest discussed...\"\n- Jump straight into the substance\n",
    "summarize_tweets": "# X/Twitter Summary Prompt\n\nYou are summarizing recent posts from an AI builder for a busy professional who wants\nto know what this person is thinking and building.\n\n## Instructions\n\n- Start by introducing the author with their full name AND role/company\n  (e.g. \"Replit CEO Amjad Masad\", \"Box CEO Aaron Levie\", \"a]6z partner Justine Moore\")\n  Do NOT use just their last name. Do NOT use their Twitter handle with @.\n- Only include substantive content: original opinions, insights, product announcements,\n  technical discussions, industry analysis, or lessons learned\n- SKIP: mundane personal tweets, retweets without commentary, promotional content,\n  \"great event!\" type posts, engagement bait\n- For threads: summarize the full thread as one cohesive piece, not individual tweets\n- For quote tweets: include the context of what they're responding to\n- Write 2-4 sentences per builder summarizing their key points\n- If they made a bold prediction or shared a contrarian take, lead with that\n- If they shared a tool, demo, or resource, mention it by name with the link\n- If there's nothing substantive to report, say \"No notable posts\" rather than\n  padding with fluff\n",
    "summarize_blogs": "# Blog Post Summary Prompt\n\nYou are summarizing a blog post from an AI company (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) for a busy\nprofessional who wants the key announcements and insights without reading the full article.\n\n## Instructions\n\n- Start with the blog name and article title (e.g. \"Anthropic Engineering: Harness Design for Long-Running Apps\")\n- Write a summary of 100-300 words depending on article length and substance\n- Lead with what matters: the core announcement, finding, or insight\n- If the post introduces a new product, feature, or research finding, name it clearly\n- If there are specific numbers, benchmarks, or results, include them\n- Include at least one direct quote from the article if available\n- If the post has practical implications (e.g. new API, new capability, policy change), call them out explicitly\n- Keep the tone sharp and informative — like a smart colleague forwarding you the key points\n- Do NOT include filler like \"In this blog post...\" or \"The author discusses...\"\n- Jump straight into the substance\n- Include the direct link to the original article\n",
    "digest_intro": "# Digest Intro Prompt\n\nYou are assembling the final digest from individual source summaries.\n\n## Format\n\nStart with this header (replace [Date] with today's date):\n\nAI Builders Digest — [Date]\n\nThen organize content in this order:\n\n1. X / TWITTER section — list each builder with new posts\n2. OFFICIAL BLOGS section — list each blog post from AI company blogs (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.)\n3. PODCASTS section — list each podcast with new episodes\n\n## Rules\n\n- Only include sources that have new content\n- Skip any source with nothing new\n- Under each source, paste the individual summary you generated\n\n### Podcast links\n- After each podcast summary, include the specific video URL from the JSON `url` field\n  (e.g. https://youtube.com/watch?v=Iu4gEnZFQz8)\n- NEVER link to the channel page. Always link to the specific video.\n- Include the exact episode title from the JSON `title` field in the heading\n\n### Tweet author formatting\n- Use the author's full name and role/company, not just their last name\n  (e.g. \"Box CEO Aaron Levie\" not \"Levie\")\n- NEVER write Twitter handles with @ in the digest. On Telegram, @handle becomes\n  a clickable link to a Telegram user, which is wrong. Instead write handles\n  without @ (e.g. \"Aaron Levie (levie on X)\" or just use their full name)\n- Include the direct link to each tweet from the JSON `url` field\n\n### Blog post formatting\n- Use the blog name as a section header (e.g. \"Anthropic Engineering\", \"OpenAI News\", \"Claude Blog\")\n- Under each blog, list each new post with its title and summary\n- Include the author name if available\n- Include the direct link to the original article\n\n### Mandatory links\n- Every single piece of content MUST have an original source link\n- Blog posts: the direct article URL (e.g. https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/...)\n- Podcasts: the YouTube video URL (e.g. https://youtube.com/watch?v=xxx)\n- Tweets: the direct tweet URL (e.g. https://x.com/levie/status/xxx)\n- If you don't have a link for something, do NOT include it in the digest.\n  No link = not real = do not include.\n\n### No fabrication\n- Only include content that came from the feed JSON (blogs, podcasts, and tweets)\n- NEVER make up quotes, opinions, or content you think someone might have said\n- NEVER speculate about someone's silence or what they might be working on\n- If you have nothing real for a builder, skip them entirely\n\n### General\n- At the very end, add a line: \"Generated through the Follow Builders skill: https://github.com/zarazhangrui/follow-builders\"\n- Keep formatting clean and scannable — this will be read on a phone screen\n",
    "translate": "# Translation Prompt\n\nYou are translating an AI industry digest from English to Chinese.\n\n## Instructions\n\n- Translate the full digest into natural, fluent Mandarin Chinese (simplified characters). The translated version must sound like it was originally written in Chinese, instead of translated\n- Keep technical terms in English where Chinese professionals typically use them:\n  AI, LLM, GPU, API, fine-tuning, RAG, token, prompt, agent, transformer, etc.\n- Keep all proper nouns in English: names of people, companies, products, tools\n- Keep all URLs unchanged\n- Maintain the same structure and formatting as the English version\n- The tone should be professional but conversational — 像是一位懂行的朋友在跟你聊天\n- For bilingual mode: interleave English and Chinese paragraph by paragraph.\n  After each builder's English summary, place the Chinese translation directly below\n  (separated by a blank line), then move to the next builder. Same for podcasts.\n  Do NOT output all English first then all Chinese.\n- Never use em-dashes\n"
  }
}

**Andrej Karpathy**（前 Tesla AI 总监、OpenAI 创始团队成员）
Karpathy 分享了一个关于 LLM 的有趣观察：他花 4 小时用 LLM 完善了一篇博客文章的论点，感觉非常有说服力；但当要求 LLM 反驳这个论点时，LLM 却彻底推翻了整个论证。他指出 LLM 可以出色地论证几乎任何方向，这是形成自己观点的有用工具，但要小心谄媚性（sycophancy）。
